methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 25 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Astrolight is a seed-stage space & aerospace company founded by Laurynas Mačiulis that has raised €2.8M to date. The valuation landscape for early-stage space ventures includes 5 public comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers spanning the sector, though direct peer multiples are constrained by heterogeneity and disclosure limitations at the seed stage.

Two standard triangulation methods prove inapplicable here. Peer-median analysis cannot proceed: while 25 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, the narrow filters (same-stage, adjacent-stage, similar-valuation-tier) yield fewer than the 3 datapoints required for statistical reliability, making an all-sector median misleading. Public-comp anchoring is likewise blocked by the absence of target revenue data—standard for private companies—which prevents application of the sector's median P/S multiple of 42.9x across the 6 public tickers identified.

Without revenue disclosure or a sufficient peer cohort at matching stage and scale, conventional multiples-based valuation cannot be triangulated. Any defensible estimate would require either: disclosure of Astrolight's current revenue run-rate or committed customer contracts; or manual scenario modeling tied to specific technical milestones and addressable market assumptions. The €2.8M raised provides a floor reference but does not itself imply valuation. Further diligence on commercial traction and technical de-risking is necessary before establishing a credible range.
